concept: Behind / In Back Of / At the Rear Of
description: Marks spatial position on the rear-facing side of a reference point. Where 'neo' indicates the presented face, 'noe' indicates the sheltered side—what lies in the wake, in the shadow, at the back. This preposition acknowledges that behind-ness carries its own qualities: protection, privacy, aftermath, the space where what is public becomes hidden.
sound_symbolism: The word begins with 'n', the same grounding nasal as its partner 'neo', establishing spatial relationship through firm articulation. But where 'neo' opens from 'e' to 'o', 'noe' reverses this: the encompassing 'o' comes first, suggesting enclosure, containment, the rounded back of things that shelters what lies within. This closed quality then trails into 'e', the sound moving away, diminishing, like the wake behind a passing boat. The vowel reversal from 'neo' makes the front/back axis audibly symmetric—hear the word and you know its orientation. Speaking 'noe' physically enacts following, as the sound recedes from rounded fullness to thinner departure.
grammatical_notes: Functions as a spatial preposition indicating position on the rear-facing side of the reference object. Precedes its object: 'noe themo' (behind the thing), 'noe himo' (behind the person). Like its partner 'neo', requires that the reference point have an inherent or contextual front/back distinction. Does not imply hidden or concealed—mere position, not visibility. For 'hidden behind' semantic emphasis, combine with appropriate modifiers.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: What lies behind is not absent but merely not facing us. 'Noe' marks position without judgment—behind is neither inferior nor hidden, simply oriented differently. This spatial neutrality reflects acceptance of all positions without hierarchical preference.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: The neutral marking of 'behind' prevents the negative associations that often cling to back-ness in other languages. Neither scheming nor inferior, 'noe' simply indicates spatial relationship, supporting clear description without embedded judgment.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Traditional knowledge values the protected rear—the back garden, the lee side of the mountain, the sheltered harbor. 'Noe' carries this understanding that behind-ness serves practical purposes of shelter and privacy rather than suggesting lesser status.
